title: Monsoon 2026 LIVE: Southwest monsoon arrives in Kerala, says IMD
content: The India Meteorological Department issues orange alert for six districts as heavy rains hit parts of Kerala

title: Complaint filed against Mamata Banerjee for linking Centre with Bangladesh political killing
content: The complaint was filed on June 3 by advocate Rinki Chatterjee Singh of the Calcutta High Court’s Jalpaiguri Circuit Bench at a police station in Siliguri; she claimed that Ms. Banerjee allegedly made ‘provocative and inflammatory statements’ against Constitutional institutions during the Assembly polls as well

title: BJP reacts to Rahul Gandhi’s remarks against PM Modi
content: Rahul Gandhi claimed that Mr. Modi will not be the Prime Minister in a year’s time, as the “system that he once controlled is now shaken and collapsing internally”

title: Why football’s most passionate fans may be in India
content: In Kerala, West Bengal and several other places across India, the FIFA World Cup is a homecoming — of fan clubs, midnight screenings, bike rallies and WhatsApp groups that spring back to life every four years

title: Kerala’s UDF govt requests CBI probe into controversial death of Kannur ADM Naveen Babu
content: Officials say Cabinet decision is based on the bereaved family’s petition. Kerala government notifies Centre of request under Section 6 of Delhi Special Police Establishment Act

title: CM Adityanath inaugurates eastern U.P.’s first flatted factory complex in GIDA
content: The CM also said, outstanding achievements in development, industry and employment are possible only when a good government is in place

title: Heavy rainfall alert in six Telangana districts on June 5
content: According to IMD’s latest bulletin, heavy rainfall is very likely to occur at isolated places in the Rangareddy, Sangareddy, Medak, Kamareddy, Mahabubnagar and Nagarkurnool districts

title: Congress raises questions on 'substantial' LIC stake in firm under SEBI scanner
content: How could LIC have missed such a huge fraud taking place in a company in which it has a substantial stake, the Congress leader asked

title: TDP launches State-wide bicycle campaign for World Environment Day; Naidu to ride in Vizag
content: TDP launches a State-wide bicycle campaign for World Environment Day; Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu will ride a bicycle in Visakhapatnam on June 5 and has urged people to cycle and plant saplings

title: TMC councillor of Kolkata Municipal Corporation arrested for ‘molesting’ woman
content: Biswajit Mondal, the councillor of Ward No. 114, was arrested following a complaint lodged by the woman from the Purba Putiary area at Regent Park police station

title: Kerala govt’s White Paper on fiscal health seeks revamp of loss-making public sector enterprises
content: Document calls for reform of KSEB, KSRTC and KWA, recommends merger of Kerala State Beverages Corporation and Kerala State Civil Supplies Corporation (Supplyco)

title: Man dies after elephant attack in Gudalur
content: Forest department officials said that he was stable until Wednesday (June 3) evening. However, his condition deteriorated and he died from his injuries at around 1.30 a.m on Thursday morning

title: Patna Kalam revival: How Bihar is bringing back the lost art that captured everyday India
content: Bihar is seeing renewed interest in the nearly forgotten 18th-century art form, in which Mughal miniature met European naturalism

title: Tamil Nadu receives surplus Cauvery water for yet another year
content: During June 2025 to May 2026, the State recorded, at Billigundulu, about 330 thousand million cubic feet (tmc ft.) against 177.25 tmc ft. prescribed in the Cauvery Water Disputes Tribunal’s final award of 2007, which was amended by the Supreme Court in its 2018 judgment

title: Sudanese national with fever detected at Hyderabad airport amid Ebola scare, shifted to Gandhi Hospital
content: Health authorities clarified that the passenger has not been classified as a suspected Ebola patient and is undergoing precautionary testing owing to his travel history and symptoms

title: Ruined education system for past 12 years: Rahul Gandhi slams PM after NEET aspirant’s death
content: His attack on the Modi government followed reports of Akanksha Chaturvedi, a NEET aspirant, found dead after falling into depression over NEET-UG exam irregularities

title: Kerala Assembly: KSRTC to expand services to northern districts to address regional imbalances, says Transport Minister C.P. John
content: Minister says that in the context of UDF government’s plan to provide free bus travel for all women on KSRTC buses, it is important that people from all regions receive its benefit

title: India set for $2-billion drone order in biggest buy, industry body says: report
content: The plans are ⁠in advanced stages with deliveries expected over 18 to 24 months, for a jump in value from recent government orders worth 30 billion rupees ($313 million) for tactical-class drones

title: Eviction of Malayidamthuruth Dalit families: Kerala HC grants govt two more weeks to resolve impasse
content: HC restrains Perumbavur munsiff court from passing orders till June 16 on the families’ eviction from 2.5 acres of land, the ownership of which has been claimed by a private individual

title: World Environment Day 2026 : Exploring the tree flora at Lok Bhavan Kerala
content: Lok Bhavan, the Kerala Governor’s official residence that stands on 12 hectares, is home to over 180 species of trees and plants

title: Modi government in panic mode on current economic situation: Congress
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh claimed the "real problem" is that private corporate investment is very tepid within India

title: Naidu calls June 4 an unforgettable day in Andhra Pradesh’s history
content: Marking the 2024 verdict, Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu calls June 4 an unforgettable day for Andhra Pradesh and says the coalition reaffirms welfare and development

title: ‘Killer’ movie stuntman dies as cylinder explodes during shoot in Otteri
content: A large number of crew members were transferring carbon dioxide gas from a large cylinder to a smaller one for a fight sequence set within a ring of fire. During the transfer, one of the cylinders exploded, killing stuntman Madhan

title: Kerala man’s death sparks allegations of surgery on wrong kidney at MCH
content: The condition of Rijith, 45, from Vanimel in Kozhikode, worsened after a keyhole surgery was performed in August 2025 to remove kidney stones

title: Tamil Nadu government signs ₹18,600-crore investment pact with L&T
content: The projects covered under the MoU include a data centre expansion project in Kancheepuram district with an investment of ₹15,000 crore

title: Man arrested over threat to Kona villagers with country-made gun
content: The Bandar Taluka police arrest G. Nageswara Rao of Kona village in Krishna district for allegedly threatening residents with a country-made gun and seize the weapon

title: Supreme Court dismisses as ‘infructuous’ WFI appeal against Vinesh Phogat’s participation in Asian Games trials
content: The top court, however, clarified that the dismissal of the appeal should not be construed as an endorsement of the adverse observations made by the High Court against the federation

title: 5 dead, others injured in Muzaffarpur hospital fire in Bihar
content: Though the cause of the incident was yet to be ascertained, the primary reports suggest that a short circuit was the reason for the fire

title: Supreme Court dismisses plea seeking guidelines for time-bound disposal of cases
content: The plea also sought formulation and implementation of a uniform national case flow management policy applicable to all courts in the country

title: The Hindu Huddle on Campus: ‘Social media protects people from being held accountable for actions’
content: Moderator Vishu Dev C.H., a faculty member from Amity School for Communication Enhancement and Transformation, pointed to concerns around anxiety, depression, distractions, and mental health and well-being

title: Unified Shakti smart card planned to enable seamless travel on BMTC buses and Namma Metro for women
content: Officials said that the new card is being designed on the National Common Mobility Card (NCMC) platform, a nationwide standard that allows commuters to access various public transport systems through a single smart card.

title: Chinnaswamy stadium stampede: One year on, grief still fresh for victims’ kin
content: Family members of the victims of the Chinnaswamy stadium stampede term it a dark day and say they never want such an incident to recur

title: As D.K. Shivakumar takes oath as Chief Minister, Kanakapura celebrates one of its own
content: Supporters intercepted nearly every vehicle travelling on Kanakapura Road to distribute sweets, and in no house across the region, especially in Doddalahalli, where the new Chief Minister hails from, were televisions switched off

title: This year, 1,59,151 engineering seats available in State’s engineering colleges
content: There is a 500-seat increase in government colleges, a 2,580-seat increase in private universities, a 1,695-seat increase in private colleges, and a marginal increase of 60 seats in deemed universities as per the matrix.

title: Southwest monsoon: Orange alert sounded for six districts in Kerala
content: After a delay of three days from normal onset date of June 1, southwest monsoon has set in over Kerala

title: Edappadi Palaniswami urges Tamil Nadu Governor to take steps against ‘horse-trading’ by TVK-led government
content: Former Minister ‘Agri’ S.S. Krishnamurthy and Rajya Sabha MP M. Dhanapal submit petition to the Governor

title: Sulur sexual assault-cum-murder: police get three-day custody of two accused
content: Key accused Karthi was brought to the court in an ambulance and on a stretcher as he had allegedly fractured his right arm and right leg while attempting to escape from the police on May 22.

title: Explained | When an economy built on cheap labour faces higher wages
content: The evidence showing limited or no job loss challenges the idea that cheap labour is a necessity for employment

title: Monsoon ‘likely to set in’ over Kerala around June 4: IMD
content: Upper-air cyclonic circulation off the south Kerala coast is expected to aid final push, and the IMD has forecast isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall over Kerala over the next six to seven days

title: Kerala monsoon onset: Why southwest monsoon arrival matters for India
content: The southwest monsoon onset in Kerala not only impacts local weather patterns but also plays a crucial role in shaping India's food security, agricultural GDP, and water supply across the nation.

title: TVK allots lone Rajya Sabha seat from Tamil Nadu to Congress for bypoll
content: With this, the Congress’ representation from Tamil Nadu in the Rajya Sabha is likely to increase to three, the highest in recent decades

title: Hard disk theft at TNPDCL: Data backup initiated, probe under way, says Tamil Nadu Minister Nirmalkumar
content: Speaking to journalists at the Secretariat, Mr. Nirmalkumar said several hard disks had been reported missing from the TNPDCL headquarters last month

title: Kerala’s Plus One admissions see 4.44 lakh applications
content: Maximum number of applicants from Malappuram – 82,678. Least from Idukki – 11,420. Wayanad has 11,790 applicants

title: Absolute incompetence of BJP unmasked by NFHS-6 data: Kharge
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge accused the BJP of having a five-step formula to hide its sin: burying selected data, abandoning the vulnerable, advertising “Sabka Saath” and “Amrit Kaal”, manipulating the narrative and protecting Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s PR at all costs

title: Naidu asks ONGC to speed up K-G basin projects and use CSR for villages
content: ONGC chairman Arun Kumar Singh calls on Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu, who asks the firm to speed up its Krishna-Godavari basin projects and spend CSR funds on nearby villages

title: Heavy rain brings relief in Delhi-NCR; IMD sounds 'red alert' for thunderstorm
content: Delhi-NCR experienced relief from warm and humid weather as light rain arrived, accompanied by a red alert from the IMD forecasting thunderstorms and gusty winds up to 70 kmph. Residents were advised to follow disaster management guidelines due to the possibility of hail at isolated spots.

title: For first time, Russia admits oil production has declined amid Ukraine's attacks on refineries
content: Russia has publicly acknowledged a dip in oil production due to unscheduled refinery repairs, marking the first such admission. This comes amid intensified Ukrainian drone attacks targeting Russian energy infrastructure, which have disrupted operations and pushed Moscow to increase crude exports. Despite these challenges, Russia aims to maximize exports and restore previous production levels.

title: 'I am hopeful': RCB's Krunal Pandya sets sights on India comeback after five years
content: Krunal, who played a key role in RCB's successful IPL 2026 title defence, contributed with both bat and ball during the season. He scored 226 runs at a strike rate of 145.80 and picked up 14 wickets at an economy rate of 8.41 as RCB won their second consecutive IPL title.

title: BJP counters Rahul's 'economic tsunami' warning amid Iran war: 'Stop selling panic'
content: The BJP has strongly refuted Rahul Gandhi's "economic tsunami" warning, calling it fear-mongering. Party leader Amit Malviya highlighted India's economic resilience with strong indicators like rising E-way bills and FDI, asserting the government has implemented measures to protect citizens and businesses from global shocks, contrasting it with the UPA era's economic vulnerabilities.

title: Europe raises 'Kill Switch' fears, makes all 27 countries to junk US, Chinese tech
content: Europe is forging ahead with ambitious plans to break free from reliance on US and Chinese technology, particularly in critical sectors like AI and microchips. New regulations aim to bolster "tech sovereignty" by ensuring sensitive data remains within the EU and boosting domestic semiconductor production, safeguarding against foreign digital threats and supply chain disruptions.

title: ENG vs NZ: Why England players are wearing black armbands at Lord’s
content: England players wore black armbands and observed a moment of silence before the first Test against New Zealand at Lord’s to honour former England cricketers who have died since the team’s last home international. The pre-match tributes also marked 10 years since New Zealand great Martin Crowe’s passing, with his daughter Emma ringing the traditional five-minute bell.

title: Sonia Gandhi backed Tharoor: Lalit Modi alleges Cong pressure during Kochi IPL row
content: Lalit Modi alleged that when he tried to stop the Kochi consortium from entering the IPL because of what he believed was an unfair equity arrangement involving Pushkar, the then United Progressive Alliance (UPA) government turned against him.

title: He came. He saw. He fled: Delhi hotel owner 'drove past' burning building
content: Hotel owner Lavkesh Bajaj reportedly fled a deadly south Delhi fire that claimed 21 lives, citing fear. Investigators are probing alleged building code violations and a lack of fire safety measures at the Flourish Stay B&B. The manager remains at large as authorities reconstruct the tragic events.

title: CEO of tech company arrested for supplying US-origin computer parts to Iran
content: Jamshid Ghomi, a dual US-Iranian national, has been arrested for allegedly violating US sanctions by shipping sophisticated US-origin technology to Iran.  He is accused of supplying networking, security, and encryption equipment to Iran's nuclear and military establishments, earning millions and laundering proceeds into his US accounts.

title: Bengal: Bidhannagar mayor Krishna Chakraborty resigns amid TMC turmoil
content: Bidhannagar Municipal Corporation Mayor Krishna Chakraborty has resigned from her post, citing personal reasons. She submitted her resignation letter to the BMC commissioner on Thursday. Chakraborty stated she worked for the people during her tenure and will continue to do so as a councillor, expressing gratitude to the residents.

title: ITR filing FY 2025-26: Top 10 points to check before submitting tax return
content: Reporting income/assets etc. in the ITR should be matched with Annual Information Statement (AIS). As through system checks and reconciliation runs, using technology for ITR processing might trigger even small gaps, errors or incorrect claims and may lead to prompt questions.

title: Canada warns citizens of immigration marriage fraud
content: Canada's immigration authority is warning citizens about marriage fraud when sponsoring partners.  Be wary of quick marriages, multiple past relationships, and a lack of background details.  These "relationships of convenience" aim solely for immigration, leaving sponsors with legal and financial burdens.  Immigration officers are trained to detect such fraudulent intent.

title: Akshay Kumar sells two flats in Borivali for Rs 7.1 crore: Report
content: Akshay Kumar has sold two residential units in Oberoi Sky City, Borivali (East), Mumbai, for a total of ₹7.1 crore. The larger apartment, spanning 1,101 sq ft, fetched ₹5.75 crore, while the smaller 252 sq ft unit sold for ₹1.35 crore. Both properties were acquired by Suvarna Rupeshkumar Sakpal.

title: Couple separated, lived 15 years apart: SC says amounts to cruelty to both
content: The Supreme Court has made a pivotal ruling, declaring that extended separation without reconciliation can be classified as mental cruelty, warranting divorce under the Hindu Marriage Act. This case, involving doctors who had been estranged for over 15 years, led the court to dissolve their marriage, citing mutual abandonment.

title: Israel-Lebanon war: Shaky truce holds as both sides continue to trade strikes; Beirut remains on edge
content: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to renew a ceasefire and establish security zones in Lebanon, barring Hezbollah from these areas. This US-mediated deal hinges on a complete halt to Hezbollah fire and their withdrawal south of the Litani River, with the Lebanese army taking control.

title: Rudram-II: How India's hypersonic missile uses enemy radars to destroy them
content: India is bolstering its aerial capabilities with the indigenous Rudram series of anti-radiation missiles, designed to neutralize enemy radar systems. These 'radar busters' are crucial for ensuring safe flight operations for Indian aircraft. Recent successful flight tests of the Rudram-II variant highlight India's commitment to advanced defense technology, complementing existing Russian-origin missiles.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2 The Revenge' OTT release: When and where to watch the uncut version
content: Get ready to embark on an exhilarating journey with Ranveer Singh in 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge'! This dynamic sequel, now streaming on JioHotstar, features an extended 'Raw & Undekha' edition that delves deeper into its captivating storyline and stunning world-building. Don't miss out—international audiences can also enjoy the film on Netflix for a global viewing experience. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Hazira: Why the tank plant PM Modi will visit on Friday matters
content: Prime Minister Modi's upcoming visit to L&T's Hazira plant highlights its growing significance in India's defence manufacturing. The facility, a hub for heavy engineering, has produced the K-9 Vajra artillery guns, with more on order, and the innovative Zorawar light tank. L&T is also developing a Futuristic Infantry Combat Vehicle, positioning Hazira as a key defence production centre.

title: Southwest monsoon hits Kerala; IMD warns of weak season
content: Kerala welcomes the southwest monsoon, marking the start of its four-month wet season. This year's rains are making a delayed entrance, with the India Meteorological Department predicting a deficit, estimating rainfall at only 90% of what is typically expected. Experts link this shortfall to the brewing El Niño, which may intensify by September.

title: 'Not suicide': Rahul blames 'broken system' after NEET aspirant's death; targets PM Modi
content: Rahul Gandhi slammed the Modi government over the NEET-UG paper leak, citing the tragic suicide of aspirant Akanksha. He highlighted the farmer father's sacrifices and blamed the "corrupt, broken system" for her death. Gandhi also questioned Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's continued tenure and accused PM Modi of damaging India's education.

title: ‘World's capital is looking at India’: Global investors betting big, says Piyush Goyal
content: Global investors are showing strong confidence in India's future. Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated that India is set to be the world's fastest-growing economy for more than twenty years. Companies like Hyundai and JCB have seen significant returns by investing in India. The nation is becoming a manufacturing hub, serving both its large domestic market and global customers.

title: Rajesh Exports stock tanks 5% to hit lower circuit as SEBI bans CMD
content: On the BSE, the stock fell 4.99% to Rs 104.65, reaching its daily lower limit. A similar trend was seen on the NSE, where the share price declined 4.99% to Rs 103.92, also locking into the lower circuit.

title: ‘Suffocated’ Firhad Hakim likely to quit as Kolkata mayor
content: Kolkata Mayor Firhad Hakim is reportedly considering stepping down this week, possibly Friday, after the Trinamool's defeat in the Kolkata Municipal Corporation polls. Sources suggest Hakim expressed feeling "suffocated" to party chief Mamata Banerjee, citing the KMC's perceived ineffectiveness.  This move follows the arrest and resignation of several councillors, adding to the pressure.

title: Team India breaks silence on Virat Kohli's availability for Afghanistan ODIs
content: Virat Kohli might miss the upcoming ODI series against Afghanistan. He reportedly sustained a hamstring injury during the IPL 2026 final. Assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate acknowledged this would be a significant blow. Kohli enjoyed a strong IPL season, scoring 675 runs. He is currently holidaying in Europe after Royal Challengers Bengaluru's title win.

title: How Ukraine brought the war to St Petersburg, Russia's strategic northern hub
content: As Russia hosted a significant economic forum, Ukrainian drones launched coordinated assaults on key energy and naval sites in Saint Petersburg. The incident, which left several injured, effectively confronted Moscow's narrative of resilience in the face of Western punitive measures. This operation showcases Ukraine's advancing long-range capabilities, taking aim at critical infrastructure deep within enemy territory.

title: Film producer and ex-CBFC chief Pahlaj Nihalani passes away due to liver cirrhosis
content: Veteran film producer and former CBFC chief Pahlaj Nihalani passed away on June 4, 2026, after a battle with liver cirrhosis. Known for backing early films of Govinda and Chunky Pandey, Nihalani produced hits like 'Aankhen' and 'Shola Aur Shabnam'. He chaired the CBFC from 2015 until his ousting in 2017. His final production, 'Rangeela Raja', released in 2019. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Aamir Khan confirms wedding with Gauri Spratt on July 5, the actor is currently in the US
content: Aamir Khan has confirmed his upcoming marriage to girlfriend Gauri Spratt, with the wedding set to take place on July 5th. The actor revealed that while they were initially content living together, they now feel ready to formalize their committed relationship.

title: 'He is a leader': Pandya reveals Virat Kohli's impact on dressing room
content: Virat Kohli's unwavering intensity and work ethic were instrumental in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's second consecutive IPL title in 2026.  Finishing as the franchise's top scorer, Kohli's leadership and champion mindset, even after facing early setbacks, inspired teammates like Krunal Pandya.  Pandya highlighted Kohli's consistent hunger to perform and win as a personal source of motivation.

title: Virat Kohli likely to miss Afghanistan ODIs; suspense continues over Rohit Sharma
content: Virat Kohli was in sublime form during IPL 2026, scoring 675 runs at an average of 56.25 and top-scoring with an unbeaten 75 in the title clash. He had reportedly felt discomfort in his right hamstring while batting during the final. Meanwhile, Rohit Sharma is yet to report to the BCCI's Centre of Excellence for a fitness assessment. With both players dealing with hamstring issues, the selectors could be forced to explore additional options ahead of the upcoming assignments.

title: Khata, mutation, property card: Here's what each document does
content: Buying property in India involves more than just keys and registration.  A 'khata' tracks property tax responsibility, a 'property card' details ownership history and liabilities, and 'mutation' updates government revenue records.  These crucial steps, often overlooked, ensure your ownership is officially recognized by local authorities, preventing future complications.

title: Illegal construction, locked terrace, multiple lapses: What made Delhi hotel fire rescue ops difficult
content: A Delhi bed and breakfast, operating illegally as a 25-room hotel despite a six-room license, flouted numerous safety norms. Flourish Stay lacked fire safety certificates, had a single exit, and its electronic gates and locked windows trapped guests during a devastating fire.  The probe revealed a shocking disregard for basic safety regulations.

title: On June 10, Modi will break Nehru’s record of longest elected PM stint
content: Narendra Modi is set to become the longest-serving unbroken head of government in India, surpassing Jawaharlal Nehru on June 10. Modi's tenure, beginning May 26, 2014, will reach 4,399 days, just ahead of Nehru's 4,398 days. This milestone highlights a significant shift in Indian political leadership and policy direction.

title: 10th straight defeat: PV Sindhu falls to An Se Young, crashes out of Indonesia Open
content: Sindhu, who had also gone down to the South Korean in the quarterfinals of last week's Singapore Open, lost 17-21, 14-21 in the round of 16 match.

title: Ranveer's 'Don 3' row: Producers Guild to hold fresh talks as FWICE drops non-cooperation order
content: The Producers Guild of India is mediating the 'Don 3' dispute between Ranveer Singh and Excel Entertainment after FWICE withdrew its non-cooperation directive. The Guild aims for an amicable resolution and clearer industry protocols. Excel Entertainment has cleared pending payments despite reported losses, signaling a potential shift in Bollywood's professional agreements. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Tax cuts, easier bond rules on the cards: How India plans to attract more foreign investment
content: Reports suggest that PM Narendra Modi-led Union Cabinet on Wednesday cleared an ordinance that will amend the Income Tax Act and enable the proposed tax exemption.

title: ‘Everything works in Delhi’: Hotel owner blames ‘other person’ in Malviya Nagar fire probe
content: A devastating fire at a Malviya Nagar hotel, claiming 21 lives, has revealed alleged violations including operating far beyond capacity and lacking fire safety clearance. The owner, arrested, reportedly claimed ignorance of rules and delegated operations. Investigations are underway into structural changes and ownership of multiple properties.

title: Huge fireball, roof collapse: Kuwait shares video of Iranian drone strikes on airport- watch
content: Kuwait International Airport was struck by Iranian drones, causing significant damage to a passenger terminal and resulting in the death of an Indian national and injuries to dozens. Kuwait's defense ministry reported destroying numerous missiles and drones, while the Ministry of External Affairs condemned the attack and called for an immediate cessation of such violence.

title: 'I'm not running at all': Lalit Modi on the 'fugitive' tag and whether he'll return to India
content: Former IPL chairman Lalit Modi vehemently denies being a "fugitive," asserting he has never been convicted of any crime and labels the tag as media sensationalism. He argues that his global travel demonstrates he is not evading authorities, questioning the legal basis for any potential arrest after years of investigations.

title: Fresh NEET 2026 leak scare? NTA rushes Telegram 'paper sale' claims to CyberCrime
content: The National Testing Agency has addressed claims of NEET 2026 question papers being sold on Telegram. This follows the recent cancellation of the NEET UG 2026 examination due to alleged paper leaks. Millions of students were impacted by the cancellation. The Central Bureau of Investigation is now investigating the matter. The Supreme Court has also expressed concerns about examination security

title: "US, Israel Trying To Sow Division After Decisive Blow": Mojtaba Khamenei
content: In a written message, Mojtaba Khamenei said "the malicious enemy" was seeking to "plant the seeds of doubt, despair, fear, mistrust and division" among the public.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Trade Flat Amid Negative Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices eased as Israel and Lebanon's ceasefire agreement boosted hopes for US-Iran deal.

title: Rooms In Basement, No Exit: Inside Another Property Of Delhi Hotel Owner
content: Flourish Inn, owned by Lavkesh Bajaj, was about 400 metres from Flourish Stay, where a deadly fire broke out on Wednesday, killing nine Indians and 12 foreigners.

title: Video: Bengal Civic Volunteer Hides Under Bed To Evade Cops
content: According to local sources, Shahidul Mia had allegedly been collecting money from people for a considerable period, claiming that he could help them secure houses under the government's housing programme

title: US-Iran Live Updates: After Battlefield Defeat, Enemies Now Seek To Sow Division In Iran: Mojtaba Khamenei
content: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Not Coders, The US May Be Desperate For 5.5 Lakh 'Plumbers' By Next Year
content: As many as 85% of companies in blue-collar industries say they have unfilled vacancies - even as world's most talented coders and engineers are going unemployed.

title: Behind Kim Jong Un's 'Exponential' Nuclear Ramp-Up Plan Is A US Problem
content: Experts point out that the conflict in the Middle East reinforced Pyongyang's long-standing view that it needs a nuclear arsenal to be able to defend itself against South Korea and the US.

title: Another Trinamool Mayor Quits As Crisis Deepens For Mamata Banerjee
content: A long-time associate of Trinamool Congress supremo and former Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, Krishna Chakraborty submitted her resignation to the municipal commissioner earlier in the day.

title: 'Inflated Bills, Gold Mine': Rajesh Exports And A Rs 15 Lakh Crore Question
content: Rajesh Exports 'Scam': SEBI alleged that the revenues reported at the group level were higher than the revenues that could actually be verified.

title: 'No Revenue Inflation': Rajesh Exports Rejects Rs 15 Lakh Crore Scam Charge
content: According to SEBI, the mismatch amounted to around Rs 15.15 lakh crore, making it one of the biggest alleged cases of revenue misrepresentation.

title: Adam Hamawy, US Doctor Who Served In Gaza Is On Path To US Congress
content: Hamawy’s victory secures him the Democratic nomination for New Jersey’s 12th Congressional District, a strongly Democratic seat.

title: Dress Code, No Music: 'Islam-Friendly Gym' In Kerala Sparks Debate
content: The now-deleted announcement, initially shared through the Instagram profile of 'Nawaz Muthu T', said the gym, which has been operating for the past 15 years, would undergo new changes.

title: "Party Bigger Than Positions": Karnataka Congress Chief Amid Cabinet Buzz
content: Bengaluru, June 4 (IANS) Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) President B.K. Hariprasad said on Thursday that those seeking positions and power may align with Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar, while those focused on strengthening the organisatio

title: Haryana's New EV Rule: Will Your Cab, E-Commerce Deliveries Cost More Now?
content: Smaller fleet owners may face challenges due to the new Haryana EV law because electric vehicles still involve higher upfront costs, say experts.

title: "We Are Scared": Nepal Family Relocates After Deadly Delhi Hotel Fire
content: Among the dead were nine Indian nationals and 12 foreign nationals, most of them from Liberia, Mozambique, Nigeria and Bangladesh.

title: ED summons Abhishek Banerjee in teacher recruitment case; residence security removed
content: abhishek Banerjee was asked to appear before ED’s Kolkata zonal office on June 15 . the ED launched a probe in the primary teachers’ recruitment case based on an FIR registered by the CBI against alleged irregularities in teachers’ appointments . officers removed the police kiosks, barricades, scanner machines and other surveillance equipment .

title: Experiment aborted too soon? How 'sidelined' Annamalai drifted away from BJP in Tamil Nadu
content: the former IPS officer has quit the BJP . he joined BJP in 2020 and made the party visible in the state like never before . sources close to Annamalai claim he has moved on and wants to put his own plans into action .
